Pisum sativum, or the garden pea, is a legume plant cultivated for its edible seeds. The bioactive constituents of Pisum sativum include isoflavones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been found to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Common herbal preparations made with Pisum sativum include dried pea leaves, pea seed extracts, and pea-based supplements, often used to support cardiovascular health and immune function.
